forecasts.reasoning: The probability of Beto O'Rourke running for a fourth major race in a decade after three consecutive losses (Senate 2018, Presidential primary 2020, Governor 2022) is low, as his margins have worsened and donor fatigue sets in. While the provided background headline suggests he is open to running ("if Texans want him to run... yes I will"), such statements are often standard political posturing to maintain relevance for his PAC (Powered by People) rather than a firm commitment. Furthermore, John Cornyn is generally a harder incumbent to unseat than Ted Cruz. Even if he does decide to run in 2026, he would have to announce before July 1, 2025 for this to resolve YES. Historically, while he announced his 2018 run in March 2017, the early announcement window is not guaranteed. Combining the low base rate of another run with the specific timing constraint, I estimate a 24% chance.

The base rate of India conducting a kinetic attack in Pakistan proper in any given month is extremely low. Historically, even during periods of high tension or limited conflict (e.g., Kargil), India has generally restricted its military strikes to Pakistan-administered Kashmir (PoK) to avoid crossing the nuclear threshold. The 2019 Balakot airstrike in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa was a rare exception. Assuming the Wikipedia link suggests some level of ongoing tension or hypothetical conflict in 2025, the probability of an attack happening specifically in June 2025 and specifically in Pakistan proper (Sindh, Baluchistan, Punjab, KP) remains very low. Given the lack of specific evidence confirming a hot war and the strong historical deterrents against striking Pakistan proper, I assign a low probability.

NUFORC receives the vast majority of its reports from the US and other English-speaking Western countries. Reports from African countries are generally infrequent, likely occurring roughly a few times per year. Furthermore, the question specifically asks for a "pilot report". Even if "pilot report" refers to frequent pilot misidentifications of Starlink satellites (which matches the "4 craft inline formation" example), restricting the criteria to a single month (June 2025) and a single continent (Africa) makes this a relatively unlikely event. Based on the overall low regional distribution of NUFORC sightings for Africa combined with the specific 1-month window, I estimate a 12% probability.

As I do not have access to live search up to the May 2025 cutoff, I apply the law of iterated expectations: my best estimate for the conditional probability in May 2025 is my current best estimate of the event occurring before July 1, 2025. Rippling raised a $200M Series F in April 2024, giving it plenty of runway and reducing the immediate necessity to go public. While 2025 is anticipated to be a stronger IPO year, the July 1 deadline captures only the first half of the year. Historically, mega-unicorns with fresh funding wait for an ideal window, often late in the year. Thus, there is roughly a 20-30% chance they file their S-1 or formally announce before July 2025. I settle on 25% as the most balanced estimate.

The presidential decree dissolving all political parties was issued on May 13, 2025. The deadline for reopening them is July 1, 2025, a window of less than seven weeks. Military juntas rarely reverse such drastic power-consolidation decrees within merely a few weeks, as doing so would signal significant weakness. Mali's current junta under Assimi Goïta has consistently defied international pressure (e.g., leaving ECOWAS, expelling MINUSMA, repeatedly delaying elections) and is highly unlikely to capitulate to pressure to reverse this decree so quickly. For these reasons, the probability of a reversal before July 1, 2025, is assessed to be very low.

Databricks generates substantial revenue and is well-capitalized, giving them no immediate pressure to IPO. The CEO has explicitly stated in the past a reluctance to IPO in unfavorable market conditions. While they are a prime candidate for a 2025 IPO, tech IPOs are frequently delayed, and an IPO targeted for late 2025 wouldn't necessitate a public announcement or public S-1 filing before the July 1, 2025 deadline. The base rate for specific decacorns going public in a defined ~1.5-year window typically hovers around 30-40%.

Trump primarily relies on Truth Social for his daily textual posts, reserving X for major announcements or occasional cross-posts, which significantly reduces the volume of potential hits for specific phrases. Elon Musk posts prolifically about immigration, but he typically focuses on broader terms (e.g., 'open borders,' 'illegal immigrants') or uses short affirmations ('Wow,' '!!,' 'Concerning') when reacting to posts by others. While a recent tweet from an account Musk follows highlighted the 'diversity visa,' the requirement for either Musk or Trump to use that exact two-word phrase makes this a difficult threshold to meet. Given the timeline (1-4 months), the odds of an exact phrase match are modest.
